title: There are No Horseshoes in Heaven
text: [SHORT DESCRIPTION] There Are No Horseshoes in Heaven exposes the passé ways of thinking that have impacted our horses. This book examines horses’ natural way of being and shows how small changes in our husbandry can yield big results. [PRODUCT TABS] Full description --- The Cavallo Guarantee --- Have a Finer-Boned Horse? Trek is the perfect boot! The Trek and ELB (Entry Level Boots) are the best choices for finer-boned horses. What can you do every day to make the most of your experience with your horse? There Are No Horseshoes in Heaven offers a profound and passionate exploration of past patterns, guiding you through an entertaining and thought-provoking invitation to make alternative choices. Protect your horse from pain and discomfort, maximize your riding experience and gain insight into the ‘hot spots’ for horses, through scientific evidence and real-life stories. title: Carole Herder
text: I am Carole Herder, President of Cavallo. I grew up in Edmonton, Alberta, Canada. As a lifelong lover of horses, my journey into the world of equine well-being began with curiosity and a fresh perspective. Unlike many who grew up around horses, I had no childhood exposure to these magnificent creatures. It wasn’t until adulthood, with children of my own, that I embarked on this remarkable path. My lack of preconceived notions allowed me to approach horse riding with an open mind. What I observed intrigued me: riders struggled to consistently maintain their horses’ soundness. How could such perfectly designed beings—evolving over 50 million years—be plagued by issues like navicular disease, laminitis, and other lameness? My first beloved horse, Rocky’s untimely death, made me determined to find a better solution. My research led to a startling revelation: nailing metal into hooves was causing harm. It was impeding shock absorption, blood circulation, and natural hoof function. The very hooves that were meant to support a hefty 1000 pounds of prey animal in flight were being destroyed by an archaic tradition. People were suffering losses without recourse, and the industry was clinging to unquestioned belief. With this understanding, I initiated the ‘Barefoot Movement’ towards a more educated, compassionate and humane approach to horse hoof care. The problem was that, unlike their

title: Greg Giles
text: Greg Giles, CEO of Cavallo Horse & Rider Inc., made the significant move from Australia to join The Cavallo Team, bringing with him a wealth of expertise in design, production, and the business of footwear. Greg’s extensive background in these areas has shaped his understanding of the critical importance of high-quality products for ensuring both longevity and customer satisfaction. His dedication to maintaining uncompromising standards has been a driving force behind Cavallo’s success in the field of barefoot hoof care. In 1999, as Managing Director of Old Macs, Greg began his journey into hoof boot education. During this time, he was instrumental in developing and introducing Old Mac Boots to the equine industry. His hands-on experience in creating effective, high-quality hoof boots laid the foundation for his future innovations. By 2006, Greg had expanded his expertise to Cavallo, where he applied his profound knowledge in sourcing, manufacturing, commerce, and marketing to enhance Cavallo Products and elevate the Cavallo Brand. Under Greg’s leadership, Cavallo has focused on producing the best hoof boots available, with particular attention to those required for specific conditions such as Laminitis and Navicular Disease. His commitment to natural hoof care is evident in the design and functionality of Cavallo’s products, ensuring they meet the diverse needs of horses while

title: Krystle Davies
text: Krystle’s love for horses started at a very young age and she began riding seriously at 10 years old. She joined the Cavallo team in 2009 as a customer service representative and has been with Cavallo ever since. Since then she has completed her Bachelor’s Degree in Psychology, married her wonderful partner Aaron, and has started her own hobby farm in beautiful Roberts Creek, B.C. She passionately shares Cavallo’s mission to develop and distribute innovative products of excellent quality that provide comfort, protection, support and value for money for both horse and rider. [ROLE] Customer Experience Manager

title: Jennifer Mundell
text: Jenny began working with Cavallo in 2015, left for a short time to pursue other interests but felt called back and resumed her position in 2017. Jenny handles social media, marketing and growing partnerships between Cavallo and other equine industry organizations. She spends a lot of time outdoors with her dogs, on the beach or in the beautiful BC forest. She grew up with horses but has not had her own horse as an adult, so her job at Cavallo works to fill that gap! Jenny’s job at Cavallo is tied to her passion for helping animals. Everyone at Cavallo is bonded by a deep love for animals (specifically horses), and a drive to do whatever is in their power to help horses thrive and be understood – free of pain and discomfort. [ROLE] Relations Coordinator

title: Mustad 'Double S' Loop Hoof Knife
text: [SHORT DESCRIPTION] The Mustad 'Double S' Loop Hoof Knife is ergonomically designed to be used in either your left or right hand. The well-balanced hoof knife has a comfortable, ergonomically designed handle. The blade exits in line with the lower part of the handle, allowing pinpoint accuracy while trimming the sole. The hook has a smaller radius, making it easier to trim between the frog and the bar. The small hook also makes it easier to form smaller holes in the hoof when removing abscesses. The blade is made of the most suitable knife steel, and it is hardened and tempered for high performance and durability. The well-balanced hoof knife has a comfortable, ergonomically designed handle. title: Yvonne Welz
text: Yvonne is a lifelong horsewoman with over 30 years of equine management and training experience. Formerly a competitive dressage rider, she schooled many horses up to an advanced level and achieved national awards with them. Always focused on learning, she studied the art of classical horsemanship for over 25 years. Professionally, she was an Advertising Art Director, until fate intervened when her beloved champion dressage mare foundered in the summer of 1999. The journey to save that mare altered the course of her life, and from then on, Yvonne was completely devoted to the barefoot horse movement. Through founding The Horse’s Hoof Magazine and website in 2000, Yvonne was instrumental in helping to introduce the methods of natural barefoot horse care to the USA. That publication, which celebrates its 20th-anniversary issue this year, is devoted to barefoot hoof trimming, natural horse care, bitless riding, and ethical horse care topics (available as a collection of online back issues.) Yvonne’s number one passion in life is helping horse owners and their horses stay healthy and happy – so joining the Cavallo Team was simply a “natural” for her! For years now, she has used Cavallo Hoof Boots on her own horses every single day, and can testify about the superior comfort and protection they provide on hard, rocky Arizona desert ground. Yvonne lives in Queen Creek, Arizona with h

title: Brigitta Luettge
text: Brigitta has always loved horses, and ALL animals, for that matter. And our customers tell us it shows! She has gone through many recent life changes that led her to her niche here, at Cavallo. Brigitta and her life partner, Andrew, recently sold their iconic Sunshine Coast restaurant after ten years of creating incredible food. They also just purchased an acreage (for her two gorgeous horses) very near the Cavallo offices. Word got out that Brigitta was nearby and had some time on her hands, so Carole quickly reached out to invite her to become part of the Cavallo Customer Service Team. Brigitta brings along 28 years of experience in dealing with every horsey matter known to man, after owning the SweetWater Ranch, a horse-riding business in Whistler, BC. She is happy to support products because she truly believes in Cavallo Boots and the comfort they offer her horses. [ROLE] Customer Experience Expert

title: Heidi Melocco
text: Heidi helps Cavallo spread the word about new products and equine wellness. She works in the horse magazine and equine publishing world—and has for over 20 years. Heidi authored Western Horseman’s Understanding Lameness, Western Horseman’s Legends 6 and 9, and Goodnight’s Guide to Great Horsemanship. She writes for several nationally-circulated horse magazines and holds first-prize awards from American Horse Publications for her writing and photography. Heidi started riding Ponies of the Americas at age five at Smiley R Ranch in Hilliard, Ohio. In college, she was president and later assistant coach of the Ohio Wesleyan University Equestrian Team. Now, she’s a registered instructor with PATH International. Melocco resides on her small-acreage horse property with her husband, Jared, daughter, Savannah, registered AQHA gelding named “Charlie,” a pony named “Romeo,” dogs “Lucy” and “Rosie,” four friendly hens, and three orange barn kitties. [ROLE] Public Relations & Press
